metadata.description: Verify and extend the deploy flow for factorial experiment champion models. The deploy flow already has 5 tasks (discover, export, import, generate, promote). This PR adds verification of the full pipeline from MLflow to BentoML serving, and extends champion metadata logging for the factorial context.
metadata.existing_infrastructure: deploy_flow.py: 5 tasks, ONNX export, BentoML import, champion promotion mlflow_wrapper.py: MiniVessSegModel + MiniVessEnsembleModel pyfunc bento_model_import.py: BentoML model import utilities deploy_artifacts.py: bentofile, docker-compose, README generation

task.name: Champion model selection from factorial results
task.description:
Extend the champion discovery logic to select the best model from the factorial experiment results. The champion is selected by:
1. Query all evaluation runs with experiment_name="factorial_evaluation_v1" 2. Rank by compound metric: 0.5*clDice + 0.5*normalize(MASD) 3. Apply fold_strategy=cv_average (standard inference mode) 4. Select the best ensemble configuration
The champion must be a single deployable artifact — either: - A single checkpoint (best single model, no ensemble) - A CV-average pyfunc (3 fold checkpoints) - A hierarchical ensemble pyfunc (multiple models)
Tag the champion in MLflow Model Registry with: - champion/model, champion/loss, champion/aux_calib - champion/post_training, champion/fold_strategy, champion/ensemble - champion/cldice, champion/masd, champion/dsc - champion/compound_metric
task.acceptance_criteria: - Selects model with best compound metric - Champion tagged with all factorial factors - Champion promoted to MLflow Model Registry - Works with all 3 artifact types (single, cv-avg, ensemble)

task.name: BentoML import chain verification
task.description:
End-to-end test of the MLflow → ONNX → BentoML import chain. Uses a mock model to verify:
1. Champion checkpoint loads from MLflow artifact store 2. ONNX export succeeds (opset 17) 3. ONNX model validates (onnxruntime inference produces correct shape) 4. BentoML model store import succeeds 5. BentoML tag is retrievable
This is a pre-deployment verification gate — if this test fails, the deploy flow should NOT proceed to serving.
task.acceptance_criteria: - ONNX export produces valid .onnx file - ORT inference matches PyTorch output (within tolerance) - BentoML import creates model in store - Model tag follows naming convention - End-to-end chain completes without error

task.name: Serving endpoint health check
task.description:
Add a health check task to the deploy flow that verifies the BentoML serving endpoint is responsive after deployment.
1. Start BentoML server (or verify it's already running) 2. Send health check request to /healthz endpoint 3. Send inference request with synthetic volume 4. Verify response shape matches (B, 2, D, H, W) 5. Log health check result to MLflow
This is a smoke test, not a full evaluation — just verifies the serving pipeline is functional.
task.acceptance_criteria: - Health endpoint returns 200 - Inference returns correct shape - Result logged to MLflow - Graceful failure if server not running (logs warning, does not crash)

task.name: Champion metadata logging for manuscript
task.description:
Log comprehensive champion metadata to MLflow for the manuscript:
- Model architecture details (param count, VRAM, FLOPs estimate) - Training details (loss, epochs, aux_calib, fold strategy) - Performance (all metrics with CIs on validation set) - Post-training details (SWA method, recalibration) - Ensemble details (strategy, n_members) - TRIPOD compliance status (link to compliance matrix) - Deployment details (ONNX opset, BentoML tag, Docker image)
This metadata feeds into the biostatistics flow's lineage manifest and the manuscript's model specification section (TRIPOD 22).
task.acceptance_criteria: - All metadata fields present and non-null - Architecture fields include param_count and vram_gb - Performance fields include all 3 primary metrics with CIs - TRIPOD compliance link points to compliance matrix
